steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Inferences. The passage establishes that time spent obtaining a gift signals thoughtfulness, then notes digital cards are easier to obtain online while 94.8% of surveyed participants found physical cards more socially acceptable to give. The blank must explain what this finding “suggests” about givers’ assumptions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘gift givers likely overestimate the amount of effort required to use digital gift cards’: the passage’s thoughtfulness norm is about effort to obtain, not effort to use — this substitutes the wrong variable. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘physical gift cards are likely preferred by gift recipients because of tangibility’: the passage directly states recipients prefer digital cards for usage, so this contradicts the text. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘physical gift cards are less desirable to recipients because they require unnecessary effort to obtain’: reverses the text — it is digital cards that are easy to obtain, and the passage says recipients prefer digital, not that they dislike physical due to effort. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘gift givers likely perceive digital gift cards as requiring relatively low effort to obtain and thus wrongly assume gift recipients will appreciate them less’: follows directly from the norm (low obtain-effort = less thoughtful) and explains why givers favor physical cards despite recipients preferring digital. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
